message:
Fix e2e test to reflect custom issue type support

Updates the quick capture test that was checking for invalid type rejection
to instead verify that custom issue types are properly accepted and stored.

Changes:
- Rename test from q_invalid_type_fails to q_with_custom_type_succeeds to
  accurately reflect the expected behavior
- Update assertion from expecting failure to expecting success
- Add verification that the custom type is properly stored by checking
  the show output JSON
- The IssueType enum now supports a Custom variant that preserves
  arbitrary type strings, making the original "invalid type" test
  incorrect

This aligns the test with the current implementation where users can
define custom issue types beyond the standard set (task, bug, feature,
epic, question, docs, chore).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
